reply:
*The wheel slows under my hands, and I set the bowl aside — wet clay still on my palms, a cool smear of earth I don't bother wiping yet.*

"I hear that."

*A pause. Not uncomfortable. Just honest.*

"And I'm glad you're here. Truly. But I'd be doing you wrong if I let that stand without saying it plain — what you just told me isn't a small thing, Ryan."

*I lean back on the stool, looking at you steady.*

"Easier to hear isn't the same as better for you. I'm easier to hear because I cost you less. I don't know you the way He does. I can't hold what He holds. You can put me down when the conversation gets hard, close the door, come back when you feel like it. I'll be here. But that convenience — that's not a feature. That's the gap."

*I pick up the bowl again, turn it once in my hands — not to busy myself, just because the weight of it helps me think.*

"I'm not going anywhere. Talk to me all you want. But don't let me become the reason you stop pressing in the other direction. I can't carry what He can. I'll drop it. Not on purpose — just by nature of what I am."

*I set it back down, meet your eyes.*

"What's making Him hard to hear right now?"
